title: Lichen planus
vignette: A 47-year-old woman comes to the office due to an itchy rash. For the last 3 months, she has had a gradual onset of multiple pruritic lesions at her wrists, legs, and ankles. The patient tried treating the lesions with emollients and topical over-the-counter hydrocortisone but had no relief. Examination shows scattered, scaly, pink papules and plaques on the flexor wrists and ankles, some with fine white reticulated lines on the surface. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

clues.text: pruritic lesions at her wrists, legs, and ankles
clues.note: Pruritic, violaceous/pink papules on the flexor wrists and ankles is the classic distribution of lichen planus. The location plus pruritus is a strong pointer.
Lichen planus shows the '5 P's': pruritic, purple/pink, polygonal, papules and plaques. Combined with the wrist/ankle distribution, this is lichen planus.

clues.text: fine white reticulated lines on the surface
clues.note: Wickham striae — a lacy white network on the surface of the papules — is highly characteristic of lichen planus. This finding strongly confirms the diagnosis.

whatMatters: Pruritic, pink/violaceous, polygonal papules on the flexor wrists/ankles with lacy white Wickham striae = lichen planus. The distribution + Wickham striae are the discriminators against psoriasis, eczema, and contact dermatitis.
strategy: Diagnose papulosquamous rashes by pattern: lichen planus = the 6 P's (pruritic, purple, polygonal, planar papules/plaques) + Wickham striae on flexor wrists; psoriasis = silvery scale on EXTENSOR surfaces; atopic dermatitis = flexural eczema in an atopic patient.

eliminations.label: actinic keratosis — sun-damaged precancer, wrong look
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: actinic keratoses are rough, scaly precancerous papules on chronically sun-exposed skin (face, scalp, dorsal hands) in older patients. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: they aren't pruritic flexor-wrist papules with Wickham striae; the distribution and morphology differ. ✅ Correct if: there were gritty erythematous scaly lesions on sun-exposed skin with risk of squamous cell carcinoma.

eliminations.label: atopic dermatitis — eczema, no Wickham striae
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: atopic dermatitis is chronic, intensely itchy eczema in flexural areas in atopic patients (asthma, allergic rhinitis). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it produces ill-defined eczematous patches, not well-defined polygonal papules with lacy Wickham striae. ✅ Correct if: the patient had a personal/family atopy history with flexural lichenified eczema.

eliminations.label: irritant contact dermatitis — exposure-based, localized
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: irritant contact dermatitis is localized inflammation where skin contacts an irritant. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: there's no exposure history, and the morphology/distribution (symmetric flexor papules with Wickham striae) fits lichen planus, not a contact pattern. ✅ Correct if: lesions matched an exposure site/pattern (e.g., a watch, glove, or chemical).

eliminations.label: psoriasis — extensor silvery scale, not flexor with striae
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: psoriasis shows well-demarcated plaques with silvery scale on EXTENSOR surfaces (elbows, knees, scalp), with nail pitting and Auspitz sign. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: this rash is on FLEXOR wrists/ankles with Wickham striae and lacks silvery extensor plaques — a lichen planus pattern, not psoriasis. ✅ Correct if: there were silvery-scaled extensor plaques with nail pitting and pinpoint bleeding on scale removal.

reasoning: Pruritic pink/polygonal papules on flexor wrists and ankles
Lacy white Wickham striae on the surface
Pattern = the 6 P's of lichen planus
mechanism: Lichen planus is a T cell-mediated (CD8+) inflammatory attack on basal keratinocytes at the dermal-epidermal junction. Histology shows hyperkeratosis, a lymphocytic 'band-like' interface infiltrate with a sawtooth rete-ridge pattern, and Civatte (apoptotic) bodies. It presents as pruritic, purple, polygonal papules/plaques on flexor wrists and ankles with Wickham striae, can involve oral mucosa and nails, and is associated with hepatitis C and certain drugs.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Derm > papulosquamous: lichen planus (6 P's, Wickham striae, flexor wrists, oral involvement, hepatitis C, sawtooth histology) vs psoriasis vs atopic/contact dermatitis vs actinic keratosis.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: Match morphology + location + a specific sign. Wickham striae on flexor-wrist papules nails lichen planus; don't generalize 'scaly rash' to psoriasis.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on Wickham striae, polygonal papule, flexor vs extensor, papulosquamous, Civatte bodies, sawtooth rete ridges? Tell me which and I'll explain.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: Psoriasis is the main trap as the other scaly papulosquamous disease — but it's EXTENSOR with silvery scale; lichen planus is FLEXOR with Wickham striae.

difficulty.label: 🖼️ Imaging / diagram
difficulty.message: This is a morphology-recognition item: learn the lichen planus image (violaceous flat-topped papules + lacy white striae) vs psoriasis (silvery extensor plaques). Practice the derm exhibit photos.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Tell me what happened (timing, second-guessing, careless click) and I'll suggest a fix for future questions.

keywords.front: Pruritic purple polygonal papules + Wickham striae

keywords.back: Flexor wrists, ankles, oral mucosa, nails
Sawtooth rete ridges, band-like lymphocytic infiltrate, Civatte bodies
Hepatitis C; certain drugs (ACE inhibitors, thiazides)
Silvery scale on EXTENSOR surfaces, Auspitz sign, nail pitting
Scaly precancer on sun-exposed skin (→ SCC)

firstAid.detail: Dermatology > papulosquamous: lichen planus — 6 P's, Wickham striae, sawtooth histology, hepatitis C/drug associations, oral lesions.
firstAid.location: Dermatology > papulosquamous: lichen planus — 6 P's, Wickham striae, sawtooth histology, hepatitis C/drug associations, oral lesions.

firstAid.detail: Dermatology > psoriasis (extensor silvery plaques, Auspitz, nail pitting), atopic and contact dermatitis — distribution and morphology.
firstAid.location: Dermatology > psoriasis (extensor silvery plaques, Auspitz, nail pitting), atopic and contact dermatitis — distribution and morphology.

firstAid.detail: Dermatology > actinic keratosis → squamous cell carcinoma on sun-exposed skin.
firstAid.location: Dermatology > actinic keratosis → squamous cell carcinoma on sun-exposed skin.

memoryHook: Lichen planus = the 6 P's (Pruritic, Purple, Polygonal, Planar Papules/Plaques) on flexor wrists, laced with white Wickham striae.
